description: Pure TDD principles translated to AI-native, machine-actionable governance rules (derived from early Beck and Martin TDD literature).

principles.title: Test-First Gate
principles.source: Kent Beck — Test-Driven Development by Example
principles.intent: Let need (a failing test) drive production code; prevent speculative implementation under pressure.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Writing tests first feels slower because it front-loads thinking and blocks 'just code it' impulses. AI cost shift: AI can draft tests rapidly, explore edge cases, and validate failure on prior code automatically. Governance: Require a RED artifact (failing test evidence) before production diffs are accepted.

constraints.description: Introduce or modify a test that fails on the pre-change code state before adding production behavior
constraints.enforcement_hint: Engine records a RED checkpoint by running tests against the previous revision

constraints.description: Accept a production-only change that introduces new behavior without a corresponding failing test
constraints.enforcement_hint: Reject patches where non-test diffs add new branches/paths without new/updated tests

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'Test-First Gate' as defined by the canonical quote: Don’t write a line of new code unless you first have a failing automated test.

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

principles.title: Eliminate Duplication
principles.source: Kent Beck — Test-Driven Development by Example
principles.intent: Continuously remove duplication so the design stays flexible and change-friendly.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Refactoring and de-duplication get deprioritized under deadlines because benefits are delayed and invisible. AI cost shift: AI can refactor with exhaustive regression and search, reducing the opportunity cost of cleanup. Governance: Gate on duplication metrics and require extraction when thresholds are exceeded.

constraints.description: Keep duplication below a configured threshold in the changed code
constraints.enforcement_hint: Run duplication detector on the diff; block or require waiver when exceeded

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'Eliminate Duplication' as defined by the canonical quote: Eliminate duplication.

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

anti_patterns.name: Copy-Paste Proliferation
anti_patterns.description: Duplicating logic instead of extracting shared intent
anti_patterns.detection_hint: Near-duplicate blocks detected across changed files

principles.title: Organic Design via Feedback
principles.source: Kent Beck — Test-Driven Development by Example
principles.intent: Let small, running increments steer design; avoid big up-front design commitments made under uncertainty.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Humans stop running tests frequently when feedback is slow or context switching is costly. AI cost shift: AI can run checks continuously and in parallel, making frequent feedback near-zero marginal cost. Governance: Gate on mandatory CI per patch and feedback-latency SLOs.

constraints.description: Run relevant tests on every patch/iteration and record results
constraints.enforcement_hint: Pre-merge gate requires a test run artifact for each patch

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'Organic Design via Feedback' as defined by the canonical quote: You must design organically, with running code providing feedback between decisions

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

anti_patterns.name: Feedback Skipping
anti_patterns.description: Batching many edits before running tests/checks
anti_patterns.detection_hint: Multiple patches without recorded test run artifacts

principles.title: Developer-Owned Tests
principles.source: Kent Beck — Test-Driven Development by Example
principles.intent: Keep feedback loops in the developer’s hands so cadence doesn’t break under delivery pressure.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Humans drift toward larger steps and shortcuts because attention and time are scarce. AI cost shift: AI can iterate in tiny steps with automated verification after each patch. Governance: Turn the principle into a pass/fail policy gate rather than relying on willpower.

constraints.description: Demonstrate the principle via a verifiable CI check for each change
constraints.enforcement_hint: Add or reference a policy rule that yields a pass/fail signal

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'Developer-Owned Tests' as defined by the canonical quote: You must write your own tests, since you can’t wait twenty times a day for someone else to write a test

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

principles.title: High Cohesion, Loose Coupling
principles.source: Kent Beck — Test-Driven Development by Example
principles.intent: Shape design so small parts are testable and changeable; resist entanglement that slows teams later.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Humans drift toward larger steps and shortcuts because attention and time are scarce. AI cost shift: AI can iterate in tiny steps with automated verification after each patch. Governance: Turn the principle into a pass/fail policy gate rather than relying on willpower.

constraints.description: Demonstrate the principle via a verifiable CI check for each change
constraints.enforcement_hint: Add or reference a policy rule that yields a pass/fail signal

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'High Cohesion, Loose Coupling' as defined by the canonical quote: Your designs must consist of many highly cohesive, loosely coupled components, just to make testing easy

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state

principles.title: Red: Write a Failing Test
principles.source: Kent Beck — Test-Driven Development by Example
principles.intent: Make the next learning step explicit; keep scope tiny and observable.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Writing tests first feels slower because it front-loads thinking and blocks 'just code it' impulses. AI cost shift: AI can draft tests rapidly, explore edge cases, and validate failure on prior code automatically. Governance: Require a RED artifact (failing test evidence) before production diffs are accepted.

constraints.description: Maintain explicit phase ordering: RED → GREEN → REFACTOR
constraints.enforcement_hint: Require phase metadata and verify fail→pass checkpoints in CI

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'Red: Write a Failing Test' as defined by the canonical quote: Red—write a little test that doesn’t work, perhaps doesn’t even compile at first

anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

principles.title: Green: Make It Work Quickly

AI cost shift: AI can iterate in tiny steps with automated verification after each patch. Governance: Enforce phase discipline with workflow rules and fail→pass checkpoints.

principles.title: Refactor: Remove the Duplication You Created

constraints.description: Keep duplication below a configured threshold in the changed code
constraints.enforcement_hint: Run duplication detector on the diff; block or require waiver when exceeded

principles.title: Design the Best API First
principles.source: Kent Beck — Test-Driven Development by Example
principles.intent: Use tests to shape the public interface from the user’s perspective, before committing internals.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Outside-in API design requires deliberate thought that teams skip when shipping quickly. AI cost shift: AI can generate consumer-style contract tests quickly, lowering the cost of outside-in design. Governance: Turn the principle into a pass/fail policy gate rather than relying on willpower.

constraints.description: Behavior must be specified via caller-facing contract tests before internal design changes
constraints.enforcement_hint: Require tests to exercise public API surface; flag tests that reach into internals

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'Design the Best API First' as defined by the canonical quote: When we write a test, we imagine the perfect interface for our operation.

constraints.description: Introduce or modify a test that fails on the pre-change code state before adding production behavior
constraints.enforcement_hint: Engine records a RED checkpoint by running tests against the previous revision

constraints.description: Accept a production-only change that introduces new behavior without a corresponding failing test
constraints.enforcement_hint: Reject patches where non-test diffs add new branches/paths without new/updated tests

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor '3 Laws: Failing Test First' as defined by the canonical quote: You must write a failing test before you write any production code.

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

principles.title: 3 Laws: Minimal Test
principles.source: Robert C. Martin — The Cycles of TDD (Clean Coder Blog)
principles.intent: Avoid over-specifying behavior early; keep the next step small and focused.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Humans drift toward larger steps and shortcuts because attention and time are scarce. AI cost shift: AI can iterate in tiny steps with automated verification after each patch. Governance: Turn the principle into a pass/fail policy gate rather than relying on willpower.

constraints.description: Demonstrate the principle via a verifiable CI check for each change
constraints.enforcement_hint: Add or reference a policy rule that yields a pass/fail signal

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor '3 Laws: Minimal Test' as defined by the canonical quote: You must not write more of a test than is sufficient to fail, or fail to compile.

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

principles.title: 3 Laws: Minimal Production Code
principles.source: Robert C. Martin — The Cycles of TDD (Clean Coder Blog)
principles.intent: Prevent “extra” implementation that becomes brittle and expensive to maintain.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Humans drift toward larger steps and shortcuts because attention and time are scarce. AI cost shift: AI can iterate in tiny steps with automated verification after each patch. Governance: Turn the principle into a pass/fail policy gate rather than relying on willpower.

constraints.description: Demonstrate the principle via a verifiable CI check for each change
constraints.enforcement_hint: Add or reference a policy rule that yields a pass/fail signal

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor '3 Laws: Minimal Production Code' as defined by the canonical quote: You must not write more production code than is sufficient to make the currently failing test pass.

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

principles.title: Micro-Cycle: Fail → Pass → Clean
principles.source: Robert C. Martin — The Cycles of TDD (Clean Coder Blog)
principles.intent: Treat cleanup as part of the cycle so structure doesn’t degrade when delivery pressure rises.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Refactoring and de-duplication get deprioritized under deadlines because benefits are delayed and invisible. AI cost shift: AI can refactor with exhaustive regression and search, reducing the opportunity cost of cleanup. Governance: Enforce phase discipline with workflow rules and fail→pass checkpoints.

constraints.description: Maintain explicit phase ordering: RED → GREEN → REFACTOR
constraints.enforcement_hint: Require phase metadata and verify fail→pass checkpoints in CI

constraints.description: All refactors must keep the full test suite green
constraints.enforcement_hint: CI blocks merge on any failure; refactor-only diffs must show unchanged contract tests

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'Micro-Cycle: Fail → Pass → Clean' as defined by the canonical quote: Clean up the mess you just made.

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

anti_patterns.name: Behavior-Changing Refactor
anti_patterns.description: Refactoring that silently alters externally observable behavior
anti_patterns.detection_hint: Contract tests change, or consumer simulations show different outputs

principles.title: Refactor Continuously (Not Later)
principles.source: Robert C. Martin — The Cycles of TDD (Clean Coder Blog)
principles.intent: Block the common failure mode: postponing refactoring until it never happens.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Refactoring and de-duplication get deprioritized under deadlines because benefits are delayed and invisible. AI cost shift: AI can refactor with exhaustive regression and search, reducing the opportunity cost of cleanup. Governance: Enforce phase discipline with workflow rules and fail→pass checkpoints.

constraints.description: Maintain explicit phase ordering: RED → GREEN → REFACTOR
constraints.enforcement_hint: Require phase metadata and verify fail→pass checkpoints in CI

constraints.description: All refactors must keep the full test suite green
constraints.enforcement_hint: CI blocks merge on any failure; refactor-only diffs must show unchanged contract tests

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'Refactor Continuously (Not Later)' as defined by the canonical quote: Refactoring is a continuous in-process activity, not something that is done late (and therefore optionally).

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

anti_patterns.name: Behavior-Changing Refactor
anti_patterns.description: Refactoring that silently alters externally observable behavior
anti_patterns.detection_hint: Contract tests change, or consumer simulations show different outputs

principles.title: FIRST: Test Quality Constraints
principles.source: Robert C. Martin — Clean Code (excerpted/quoted)
principles.intent: Make the test suite runnable and trustworthy enough that teams keep using it under time pressure.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Humans drift toward larger steps and shortcuts because attention and time are scarce. AI cost shift: AI can iterate in tiny steps with automated verification after each patch. Governance: Turn the principle into a pass/fail policy gate rather than relying on willpower.

constraints.description: Demonstrate the principle via a verifiable CI check for each change
constraints.enforcement_hint: Add or reference a policy rule that yields a pass/fail signal

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'FIRST: Test Quality Constraints' as defined by the canonical quote: Tests should be Fast, Independent, Repeatable, Self-Validating, and Timely.

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test

principles.title: Test Code Is First-Class
principles.source: Robert C. Martin — quoted in Tyler Hawkins (Better Programming, Medium)
principles.intent: Keep tests maintainable so they don’t become a drag and get skipped during delivery crunches.
principles.ai_native_interpretation: Human impracticality: Humans drift toward larger steps and shortcuts because attention and time are scarce. AI cost shift: AI can iterate in tiny steps with automated verification after each patch. Governance: Turn the principle into a pass/fail policy gate rather than relying on willpower.

constraints.description: Demonstrate the principle via a verifiable CI check for each change
constraints.enforcement_hint: Add or reference a policy rule that yields a pass/fail signal

principles.prompt_guidance: Honor 'Test Code Is First-Class' as defined by the canonical quote: Test code is just as important as production code.

anti_patterns.name: Speculative Implementation
anti_patterns.description: Adding production behavior without a test that fails on the pre-change state
anti_patterns.detection_hint: New production paths added without a corresponding new/updated failing test
